release_definition_of_done.golden_rule: All 10 release dimensions must pass before deploying. No partial success. No exceptions.

1_gate_validation.title: Gate Validation (Core Quality Gates)
1_gate_validation.description: All five core quality gates must pass in sequence without any failures or suppressions.
1_gate_validation.why_it_matters: Ensures code compiles, tests pass, is well-formatted, meets performance targets, and ready for production. A gate failure indicates the codebase is in an invalid state.
success_criteria: just timeout-check: Exit code 0 (timeout command available)
just check: Exit code 0 (no compiler errors, all 15 crates compile)
just lint: Exit code 0 (zero clippy warnings, code properly formatted)
just test: Exit code 0 (all 347+ tests pass with real collaborators, Chicago TDD)
just slo-check: Exit code 0 (all performance SLOs met: build ≤15s, test ≤30s, RDF ≤5s/1k triples, memory ≤100MB)
failure_recovery: Read compiler error (error[E...]) and identify root cause (not symptom)
Fix the code at the reported line; do NOT suppress with #[allow(...)]
Re-run the failed gate: just check / just lint / just test / just slo-check
Iterate until gate passes (may require multiple fixes per gate)
Do NOT proceed to next dimension until all five gates pass
If blocked >15 minutes on a single gate, stop and escalate (Andon signal)

validation.command: just timeout-check && just check && just lint && just test && just slo-check
validation.expected_output: Finished [unoptimized + debuginfo] / test result: ok / no warnings / benchmark metrics within SLOs

automation: .git/hooks/pre-commit → scripts/hooks/pre-commit.sh (runs just pre-commit: fmt-check + check + lint + test-lib)
.git/hooks/pre-push → scripts/hooks/pre-push.sh (runs cargo test on main branch gates)
CI gate: All five gates must pass before allowing merge to main
All gates run on every commit; failure blocks merge automatically
evidence: Exit code 0 from each gate (captured in CI logs)
Test count from just test output: 'test result: ok. NNN passed; 0 failed'
Clippy output: zero warnings, all `-D warnings` errors resolved
Benchmark output from just slo-check: all metrics ≤ thresholds
No #[allow(...)] suppressions in production code (linting verifies)
No #[ignore] on tests (ensures all tests run)
forbidden_behaviors: ❌ Silencing errors with #[allow(...)] — compiler warnings are real bugs
❌ Marking tests #[ignore] — tests must run, not be skipped
❌ Using || true on failing commands — failures must surface
❌ Leaving todo!() in production code — must be completed
❌ Proceeding despite gate failure — STOP THE LINE protocol enforced
❌ Bypassing pre-commit hook with --no-verify — gates must run

2_artifact_emission.title: Artifact Emission (Output Files Generated)
2_artifact_emission.description: Code generation pipeline (μ₁–μ₅) produces all expected output files with correct content, timestamps, and file permissions.
2_artifact_emission.why_it_matters: Proves that the code generation engine actually ran and produced deliverables. Prevents decorative completion (command exits 0 but nothing was generated).
success_criteria: ggen sync produces new files in output directories (per ggen.toml output_file patterns)
All output files have non-zero size (no empty placeholders)
File timestamps reflect current run (not stale from prior run)
File permissions are correct (executable for scripts, readable for code)
Content matches SPARQL-derived bindings (no hardcoded data)
No output files have empty or default-sentinel content (contract drift detection)
failure_recovery: Run ggen sync --dry_run true to preview expected artifacts
Compare preview output to actual ggen sync output
If files not created: check ggen.toml output_file patterns; verify SPARQL queries produce bindings
If files empty: check Tera templates for missing variables; verify SPARQL SELECT provides all columns
If files have stale content: verify μ₁ (load) stage reads current inputs; check if sync --incremental skipped hash step
Check .ggen/receipts/latest.json output_hashes for expected files

validation.command: ggen sync --audit true && ls -la $(grep -r 'output_file' ggen.toml | cut -d '=' -f2 | tr -d '"') && wc -l $(find . -name '*.rs' -newer .ggen/receipts/latest.json 2>/dev/null)
validation.expected_output: All output_file paths exist, have timestamps >= sync start time, have non-zero byte counts

automation: ggen sync validates μ₁–μ₅ pipeline completed and generated artifacts
ggen sync --audit true emits receipt with output_hashes for all generated files
ggen receipt verify checks output_hashes against actual files (detects tampering or deletion)
CI validates artifact existence and checksums match receipt
evidence: Output files exist on disk at paths specified in ggen.toml
File modification times >= ggen sync start time (from receipt timestamp)
output_hashes in .ggen/receipts/latest.json includes all generated files with SHA-256 checksums
Content spot-check: read generated files, verify they match expected schema/format
μ stage logs in receipt show pipeline progression: μ₁ (load) → μ₂ (extract) → μ₃ (generate) → μ₄ (validate) → μ₅ (emit)
forbidden_behaviors: ❌ Exit 0 but no output files created (decorative completion)
❌ Empty output files (size 0 bytes) — indicates template or binding failure
❌ Output files with stale content from prior run — contract drift
❌ Hardcoded data in output instead of SPARQL-derived bindings — epistemic bypass
❌ Missing output_hashes in receipt — prevents verification

3_checksum_validation.title: Checksum Validation (SHA-256 Integrity)
3_checksum_validation.description: All artifacts are hashed (SHA-256) at generation time, stored in receipt, and verified against actual files before deployment.
3_checksum_validation.why_it_matters: Detects artifacts that were corrupted, modified, or tampered with after generation. Enables rollback to known-good state (previous receipt hash).
success_criteria: Each output file has a SHA-256 checksum computed immediately after μ₅ (emit) stage
Checksums stored in .ggen/receipts/latest.json → output_hashes
Checksums match actual file contents (byte-for-byte verification)
Input hashes (ontology, queries, templates, manifest) recorded in input_hashes
Input hashes match actual inputs at sync start time
Checksums are cryptographically strong (SHA-256 min; no MD5 or SHA1)
Checksum verification passes before signature check (order: integrity → signature → causality)
failure_recovery: Re-run ggen sync (regenerate all artifacts from ontology)
Compare new receipt checksums to previous receipt: if same, sync is idempotent (good)
If checksums differ: identify what changed in inputs (ontology, queries, templates, manifest)
Manual verification: sha256sum $(find . -name '*.rs' -newer .ggen/receipts/latest.json) and compare to output_hashes
If file corrupted: restore from version control or previous receipt's source state
Do NOT deploy if checksums cannot be verified (data integrity at risk)

validation.expected_output: All output hashes verified OK. All input hashes verified OK. Checksums match actual files.

automation: ggen sync computes SHA-256 for all inputs (ontology, SPARQL queries, Tera templates, ggen.toml)
ggen sync stores input_hashes in receipt before pipeline runs
ggen sync computes SHA-256 for all outputs immediately after μ₅ stage
ggen sync stores output_hashes in receipt after μ₅ completion
CI gate: ggen receipt verify checks all hashes before allowing promotion to next environment
Pre-deployment: checksum verification prevents deploying corrupted artifacts
evidence: .ggen/receipts/latest.json → input_hashes: { 'ontology@version': 'sha256_hex', 'queries': '...', ... }
.ggen/receipts/latest.json → output_hashes: { 'path/to/file.rs': 'sha256_hex', ... }
sha256sum command output matches all entries in output_hashes (byte-for-byte)
Idempotence proof: run ggen sync twice, compare input_hashes and output_hashes (must be identical if inputs unchanged)
forbidden_behaviors: ❌ Empty or null checksum values in receipt — contract drift
❌ Checksums computed AFTER deployment (must be computed at generation time)
❌ Checksums omitted for any output file — prevents integrity verification
❌ Trusting checksums without verification (must byte-match actual files)
❌ Using weak hashing (MD5, SHA1) — only SHA-256 or stronger
❌ Modifying artifacts after hashing — hashes will not match

4_signature_verification.title: Signature Verification (Cryptographic Proof)
4_signature_verification.description: Receipt is digitally signed with Ed25519 private key; signature verifies against public key before deployment.
4_signature_verification.why_it_matters: Proves receipt was created by the authoritative build system (not forged or tampered). Enables audit trail and non-repudiation.
success_criteria: Receipt signature computed over JSON content (deterministic serialization)
Signature field is non-empty base64-encoded Ed25519 signature
Public key available at .ggen/keys/verifying.key
ggen receipt verify succeeds with exit code 0
Signature verification fails if receipt content modified (even 1 byte)
Signature verification fails if verifying.key is deleted or corrupted
Signature timestamp matches receipt timestamp (no backdating)
failure_recovery: ggen receipt verify .ggen/receipts/latest.json --public-key .ggen/keys/verifying.key
If signature invalid: receipt was modified after creation (data integrity compromised)
If key missing: restore from secure key backup (do NOT regenerate key, it breaks signature verification)
If timestamp mismatch: receipt may be forged (contact build system administrator)
Do NOT deploy with invalid signature — prevents audit trail

validation.expected_output: Signature valid. Receipt verified. Timestamp: <RFC-3339>. Signer: <key-fingerprint>

automation: ggen sync signs receipt with Ed25519 private key from .ggen/keys/signing.key
ggen sync computes signature over deterministic JSON (canonical form, no whitespace variation)
ggen sync stores signature in receipt → signature field (base64-encoded)
CI gate: ggen receipt verify checks signature before deploying to any environment
All archived receipts signed (enables historical verification: did this code come from our build system?)
evidence: .ggen/receipts/latest.json → signature: '<base64-encoded-signature>' (non-empty, valid base64)
.ggen/keys/verifying.key exists and is readable
ggen receipt verify exits 0 (proves signature is valid)
Audit log: which key signed this receipt (fingerprint in verify output)
forbidden_behaviors: ❌ Empty signature field (contract drift — receipt is unsigned, unverifiable)
❌ Signature not verified before deployment (permits forged receipts)
❌ Trusting receipt without checking signature (bypasses audit trail)
❌ Regenerating signing key without rotating verifying key (breaks verification)
❌ Storing private key in version control or CI logs (security risk)
❌ Hardcoded test UUID in operation_id (contract drift — not a real operation)

5_lockfile_finality.title: Lockfile Finality (.ggen/packs.lock)
5_lockfile_finality.description: Package lockfile is frozen before deployment; no further pack additions/removals allowed during sync --locked. Enables reproducible deployments.
5_lockfile_finality.why_it_matters: Prevents new dependencies sneaking into production. Ensures deployment uses exact same packs (versions, digests) as tested.
success_criteria: .ggen/packs.lock exists and is readable
.ggen/packs.lock is valid JSON with correct structure
Each pack entry has: id (non-empty), version (semver), digest (SHA-256 hex, 64 chars), installed_at (RFC-3339 timestamp)
All digest fields are non-empty (not null, not '')
All installed_at timestamps are real (not zero-value, not test fixture '2000-01-01T00:00:00Z')
ggen sync --locked reads lockfile before executing any pipeline stage
ggen sync --locked fails hard (exit 1) if lockfile missing or digest mismatch
ggen sync --locked produces receipt with locked_packs list (proves which packs were used)
failure_recovery: Run ggen packs install <pack@version> to create/update lockfile
Verify .ggen/packs.lock was created with non-empty digest and current timestamp
If lockfile corrupted: delete and reinstall all packs: rm .ggen/packs.lock && ggen packs install <all-packs>
If digest empty: fix PackRegistry to compute SHA-256 at install time (not default to '')
If timestamp stale: verify installed_at was set to current time (not copy/pasted from fixture)
Before deployment, commit .ggen/packs.lock to version control (ensures reproducibility)

validation.command: jq -e '.packages[] | select(.digest | length == 64 and test("^[0-9a-f]+$")) and (.installed_at | startswith("20"))' .ggen/packs.lock && ggen sync --locked --dry_run true
validation.expected_output: All packs in lockfile verified. Sync would proceed with locked pack set.

automation: ggen packs install updates .ggen/packs.lock atomically (all-or-nothing write)
ggen packs install computes SHA-256 digest at install time and stores in lockfile
ggen packs install sets installed_at to current RFC-3339 timestamp (not placeholder)
ggen sync --locked reads lockfile as first step (before any pipeline execution)
ggen sync --locked exits non-zero if lockfile missing (fail-open prevention)
ggen sync --locked exits non-zero if any pack digest mismatches installed file (tampering detection)
CI gate: .ggen/packs.lock must be committed before allowing deployment
evidence: .ggen/packs.lock file exists on disk with modification time >= last ggen packs install
All packages in lockfile have digest field with 64-char SHA-256 hex string
All packages have installed_at with valid RFC-3339 timestamp (recent, not test fixture)
Receipt includes locked_packs section showing which packs were locked and their digests
.ggen/packs.lock committed to git (git log --follow shows history of pack changes)
forbidden_behaviors: ❌ Lockfile with empty digest field (contract drift — digest not recorded at install)
❌ Lockfile with null digest (same as empty; prevents verification)
❌ Lockfile missing installed_at (cannot verify when pack was installed)
❌ Running ggen sync without --locked flag in production (permits undeclared pack changes)
❌ Modifying .ggen/packs.lock manually (should only be written by ggen packs install)
❌ Deleting .ggen/packs.lock without re-installing packs (ggen sync --locked will fail, blocking all deployments)

6_version_tagging.title: Version Tagging (Semantic Versioning)
6_version_tagging.description: Release is tagged in git with semantic version (major.minor.patch) matching Cargo.toml version. Enables rollback and historical tracking.
6_version_tagging.why_it_matters: Enables deployments to specific versions. Rollback to prior version by checking out prior git tag. Prevents accidental re-deployment of old code.
success_criteria: Cargo.toml [workspace.package] version matches release version (e.g., 26.6.11)
All crates in workspace have matching version (enforced via workspace.version)
git tag v<version> exists and points to the release commit
git tag is annotated (not lightweight) with release notes in message
git tag is signed with GPG key (non-repudiation)
git tag message includes: version, release date, breaking changes (if any), migration guide (if any)
git tag is pushed to remote (origin) before deployment (enables team access)
failure_recovery: Update Cargo.toml [workspace.package] version to next semver (e.g., 26.6.11 → 26.6.12 for patch, 26.7.0 for minor)
Run cargo update (updates lock files to match new version)
Create git tag: git tag -a v<version> -m 'Release v<version> - <notes>' -s (requires GPG key)
If tag created with wrong version: delete and recreate: git tag -d v<wrong> && git push origin :refs/tags/v<wrong> && git tag -a v<correct> ...
Push tag: git push origin v<version>
Verify tag exists: git tag -l v<version> && git show v<version>

validation.command: grep 'version =' Cargo.toml | head -1 | cut -d '=' -f2 && git tag -l v$(grep 'version =' Cargo.toml | head -1 | cut -d '=' -f2 | tr -d '"' | xargs) && git show v$(grep 'version =' Cargo.toml | head -1 | cut -d '=' -f2 | tr -d '"' | xargs) --format=full
validation.expected_output: version = 26.6.11 (matches tag v26.6.11 on current commit, annotated, signed)

automation: CI detects version change in Cargo.toml (diff HEAD~1 vs HEAD)
CI creates annotated, signed git tag v<version> pointing to release commit
CI pushes tag to origin before allowing deployment (prevents orphaned tags)
CD system verifies git tag exists and matches deployment version
Release notes automatically generated from git log between prior tag and current tag
evidence: Cargo.toml [workspace.package] version: 26.6.11
git tag -l output: v26.6.11
git show v26.6.11 output: annotated tag, signed with GPG, release notes in message
git log --decorate shows tag v26.6.11 on release commit
git push origin v26.6.11 succeeded (tag in remote)
forbidden_behaviors: ❌ Version in Cargo.toml doesn't match git tag (causes deployment confusion)
❌ Lightweight tag instead of annotated tag (no release notes, no signature)
❌ Unsigned tag (enables tag forgery)
❌ Tag not pushed to remote (team cannot see or verify release)
❌ Hardcoded version as string in code instead of reading from Cargo.toml (contract drift)
❌ Bumping version without committing change (CI will detect version mismatch)

7_changelog_recording.title: Changelog Recording (CHANGELOG.md / Receipt Log)
7_changelog_recording.description: All user-facing changes documented in CHANGELOG.md with version, date, and impact summary. Receipt logs all generated artifacts.
7_changelog_recording.why_it_matters: Users can see what changed between versions. Enables migration guides for breaking changes. Audit trail of feature additions and fixes.
success_criteria: CHANGELOG.md exists in repository root
Latest entry is for current release version (matches Cargo.toml version)
Entry includes: version, release date (YYYY-MM-DD), section headers (Breaking Changes, Features, Fixes, etc.)
Breaking changes explicitly listed (if any) with migration guide
New features documented with examples
Bug fixes reference issue numbers (e.g., 'Fix #123')
.ggen/receipts/latest.json records all generated artifacts with version and timestamp
failure_recovery: Add CHANGELOG.md entry: version, date, sections (Breaking Changes, Features, Fixes, Notes)
Include migration guide for any breaking changes
Reference issue/PR numbers for traceability
Commit CHANGELOG.md before release tag
If receipt missing: re-run ggen sync to generate new receipt with artifact list

validation.command: head -50 CHANGELOG.md | grep -E '^## \[26\.6\.11\]|^### Breaking Changes|^### Features|^### Fixes' && jq -e '.version and .timestamp and .output_hashes' .ggen/receipts/latest.json
validation.expected_output: CHANGELOG.md has entry for v26.6.11 with sections. Receipt has version, timestamp, and output_hashes.

automation: CI gate: CHANGELOG.md must be updated on any non-doc commit (prevents releases without notes)
CI validates CHANGELOG.md format (version headers, date format YYYY-MM-DD)
Release notes generated from CHANGELOG.md for git tag message
ggen sync records all artifacts in receipt (proof of what was generated)
evidence: Sections present: Breaking Changes (if any), Features, Fixes, Notes
.ggen/receipts/latest.json → version: '26.6.11', timestamp: '2026-06-14T...'
Receipt → output_hashes: { 'path/to/generated/file': 'sha256_hash', ... }
forbidden_behaviors: ❌ No CHANGELOG.md entry for release (users cannot see what changed)
❌ CHANGELOG entry with wrong version or date (misrepresents release)
❌ Breaking changes not documented (users blindsided by migrations)
❌ Issue/PR numbers not referenced (audit trail broken)
❌ Receipt missing version or timestamp (contract drift)
❌ Artifact-level changes not logged (cannot prove what was generated)

8_smoke_testing.title: Smoke Testing (End-to-End Validation)
8_smoke_testing.description: Generated code compiles, tests pass, runs without errors on simple test cases. Detects integration failures not caught by unit tests.
8_smoke_testing.why_it_matters: Proves generated artifacts are functionally correct and can be deployed. Unit tests prove individual components; smoke tests prove the system works end-to-end.
success_criteria: Generated code in output directories compiles without errors
Generated code lints without warnings
Generated tests run and pass (chicago TDD: real collaborators, no mocks)
Generated binaries execute successfully on simple test inputs
Output matches expected format (JSON schema, SQL schema, etc.)
No runtime panics or resource leaks (valgrind/asan for C/Rust)
Performance meets SLOs (generated code execution time, memory usage)
failure_recovery: Compile generated artifacts: cargo build (if Rust) or language-specific compiler
If compile fails: check Tera template bindings (missing variables? wrong type?)
If tests fail: verify SPARQL queries produce expected data (run queries manually against ontology)
If runtime fails: add debug logging, check for missing dependencies or incorrect file paths
If performance exceeds SLO: profile generated code, identify hot path, optimize template or ontology
Do NOT ship if smoke tests fail (indicates integration problem)

validation.command: cd $(ls -d . | head -1) && cargo build --release && cargo test --release && cargo bench --bench cli_startup_performance -- --test
validation.expected_output: Finished release. Test result: ok. Benchmark metrics within SLOs.

automation: CI smoke tests: build generated code, run tests, benchmark generated binary
Smoke tests run in isolated environment (detects dependency issues early)
Smoke tests validate output format (schema validation for JSON, SQL, etc.)
Smoke tests measure runtime and memory (detect performance regressions)
evidence: CI log: cargo build succeeded (zero compiler errors)
CI log: cargo test succeeded (all tests pass, real collaborators used)
CI log: cargo bench output (metrics within SLO thresholds)
Generated binary runs on test input and produces expected output (manual verification for first release)
forbidden_behaviors: ❌ Generated code doesn't compile (template/binding bug not caught earlier)
❌ Generated code has warnings (linting not enforced on generated code)
❌ Generated tests use mocks (violates Chicago TDD; not testing real behavior)
❌ Generated binary crashes on valid input (integration bug)
❌ Performance exceeds SLO (regression not caught)
❌ Smoke tests skipped due to time pressure (leads to deployment failures)

9_deployment_checklist.title: Deployment Checklist (Pre-Deployment Verification)
9_deployment_checklist.description: Final verification before deploying: no uncommitted changes, branch is clean, CI is green, all gates passed, rollback plan documented.
9_deployment_checklist.why_it_matters: Prevents accidental deployment of broken code, uncommitted work, or from wrong branch. Ensures team can safely revert if something goes wrong.
success_criteria: git status shows no uncommitted changes (working tree clean)
git log --oneline shows all commits are on main branch (no on-detached-head)
All CI checks pass (green checkmarks for all gates)
CHANGELOG.md is up to date (version matches release, release date is today)
.ggen/packs.lock is committed (enables reproducibility)
Prior release tag is documented (for rollback reference)
Release notes prepared (from CHANGELOG.md or git log)
Stakeholders notified of release plan (for emergency contact during rollback)
failure_recovery: git status: stash uncommitted changes if they're not part of release, or commit them
git log: ensure you're on main and all commits are pushed to origin
CI checks: re-run failed checks, identify root cause, fix and re-push
CHANGELOG.md: add missing entry with version and date
.ggen/packs.lock: commit if missing
If any check fails: DO NOT PROCEED. Go back and fix the issue.
Only deploy when all checklist items are verified green.

validation.command: git status --short && git branch -vv && git log --oneline -1 && echo '---' && cat CHANGELOG.md | head -5 && echo '---' && git diff origin/main || true
validation.expected_output: Working tree clean. On main branch. Latest commit shown. CHANGELOG.md entry present. No diff from origin/main.

automation: Pre-deployment script: verify all checklist items (fail fast if any missing)
CI gate: blocks deployment if working tree is dirty (prevents accidental commits during deployment)
CI gate: blocks deployment if on wrong branch (prevents main branch corruption)
CD system requires manual sign-off before proceeding (human gate: 'I've reviewed the checklist')
evidence: git status output: 'On branch main. Your branch is up to date with origin/main. Nothing to commit, working tree clean.'
CHANGELOG.md entry for current version with today's date
.ggen/packs.lock committed (git log shows commit with .ggen/packs.lock change)
All CI checks green (screenshot or CI log URL)
forbidden_behaviors: ❌ Deploying with uncommitted changes (code not tracked in git)
❌ Deploying from non-main branch (wrong code shipped)
❌ Deploying when CI is red (broken code deployed)
❌ Skipping CHANGELOG.md update (users unaware of changes)
❌ Deploying without rollback plan (unable to revert if issues arise)
❌ Deploying without stakeholder notification (no emergency contact for rollback)

10_rollback_plan.title: Rollback Plan (Reversibility & Recovery)
10_rollback_plan.description: Documented procedure to revert to prior version in case of deployment failure. Includes prior git tag, receipt hash, and data migration guide.
10_rollback_plan.why_it_matters: Enables rapid recovery from catastrophic failures. Proves deployment is reversible (not a one-way trip). Provides confidence to proceed with high-risk releases.
success_criteria: Prior version git tag documented (e.g., v26.6.10 is current, rolling back to v26.6.9)
Prior receipt hash available (from .ggen/receipts/ archive)
Rollback command documented and tested (git checkout v<prior> && ggen sync)
Data rollback procedure documented (if release includes schema changes)
Monitoring alarms defined (what indicates rollback is needed: error rate >X%, latency >Y%, etc.)
Rollback decision criteria documented (when to rollback vs. when to fix forward)
Rollback communication plan (who to notify, escalation path)
failure_recovery: Detect issue (high error rate, latency spike, data corruption)
Verify issue is release-related (compare metrics pre/post-deployment)
Execute rollback: git checkout v<prior-tag> && ggen sync --locked
Verify rollback successful: smoke tests pass, metrics return to normal
Notify stakeholders (issue resolved, investigating root cause for next release)
Post-mortem: identify what went wrong, add validation to prevent recurrence

validation.command: git tag -l | tail -2 && git show $(git tag -l | tail -2 | head -1) --format=fuller && echo '---' && ls -la .ggen/receipts/ | tail -5
validation.expected_output: Prior two tags shown (current and previous). Current tag points to release commit. Receipts for both versions available.

automation: Release notes include: current version, prior version (for rollback reference), breaking changes (if any)
Receipts archived (at least 3 prior releases kept in .ggen/receipts/ for rollback)
Monitoring automatically detects anomalies (error rate, latency) post-deployment
Runbook documented: 'If alerts fire during first hour post-deployment, execute rollback'
Rollback tested in staging before release (prove it works before production needs it)
evidence: ROLLBACK.md or RUNBOOK.md file documenting rollback procedure
Prior git tag(s) documented with dates and versions
.ggen/receipts/ contains receipts for multiple versions (enables jumping to any prior state)
Monitoring dashboard shows pre/post-deployment metrics (baseline for rollback trigger)
Staging runbook proves rollback works (before production deployment)
forbidden_behaviors: ❌ No rollback plan documented (unable to recover from failure)
❌ Rollback command not tested (may fail when needed most)
❌ Old receipts deleted (unable to jump to prior state)
❌ No monitoring/alerting (don't detect issues until users complain)
❌ Rollback requires manual code changes (should be push-button, <5 min)
❌ Rollback path not tested in staging (discovers problems in production)

release_definition_of_done.combined_validation_command: just timeout-check && just check && just lint && just test && just slo-check && ggen sync --audit true && ggen receipt verify .ggen/receipts/latest.json --public-key .ggen/keys/verifying.key && jq -e '.packages[] | .digest | length == 64' .ggen/packs.lock && git status --short && git log --oneline -1

automation: All five gates (1_gate_validation) run on every git push (blocks merge if any fail)
Artifact emission (2_artifact_emission) validated in CI before allowing deployment
Checksum validation (3_checksum_validation) required before downloading artifacts
Signature verification (4_signature_verification) required before promoting to next environment
Lockfile finality (5_lockfile_finality) enforced by ggen sync --locked (fails if lockfile absent or corrupted)
Version tagging (6_version_tagging) automated by CI (creates tag on release commit)
Changelog recording (7_changelog_recording) gated by CI (rejects release without CHANGELOG update)
Smoke testing (8_smoke_testing) runs in CI (blocks deployment if generated code doesn't compile/test)
Deployment checklist (9_deployment_checklist) manual gate (requires human sign-off)
Rollback plan (10_rollback_plan) documented in RUNBOOK.md (required before first production deployment)
human_gates: Gate 6 (version_tagging): Human creates annotated, signed git tag (enforced by CI)
Gate 7 (changelog_recording): Human writes CHANGELOG.md entry (enforced by CI)
Gate 9 (deployment_checklist): Human verifies all checklist items (final approval gate)
Gate 10 (rollback_plan): Human reviews and documents rollback strategy (before production)
enforcement.andon_protocol: If ANY dimension fails, STOP immediately. Do NOT proceed. Fix the failure and re-validate dimension before moving to next.
enforcement.no_partial_success: All 10 dimensions must pass. A partial pass (9/10) is a failure. A release is either READY or NOT READY. There is no middle ground.

rules: .claude/rules/coding-agent-mistakes.md — 5 mistake classes, 6-question contract, invariants, sabotage tests
.claude/rules/otel-validation.md — OTEL span verification for LLM/external services
docs: docs/DEFINITION_OF_DONE.md — 5-gate quality gates (check, lint, test, slo-check, pre-commit)
docs/architecture/COMPRESSED_REFERENCE.md — C4 diagram, production sync flow, stub registry
ROLLBACK.md or RUNBOOK.md — Rollback procedure (to be created)

summary.definition: A release is READY TO SHIP when ALL 10 dimensions pass validation and gates are green.
